title: Replace silent catch blocks with logging in loadgen
description:
## Problem
Multiple test files have silent `catch { }` or `catch { // ignore }` blocks that swallow errors. Debugging test failures is impossible when errors vanish.

## Evidence
- token_smoke.ts:75 - `catch { // ignore }`
- token_transfer_loadgen.ts:204-205 - silent balance parse failure
- Similar patterns in 5+ other files

## Fix
Replace all silent catches with structured logging:
```typescript
// Before
catch { }

// After
catch (err) {
  console.error('[loadgen] Non-critical error in X:', err instanceof Error ? err.message : String(err))
}
```

## Why
Makes test failures diagnosable. Currently when a test fails, there's no trace of what went wrong.
close_reason: Added structured non-critical logging across loadgen ramps, IM handlers, peersync fetch helpers, GCR cleanup, and parser fallbacks

title: Bug hunting
close_reason: Stale umbrella epic with no active tasks; current bugs remain tracked directly

title: Investigate token transfer loadgen regression on current local devnet
close_reason: Token is excluded from the active-core baseline after implementation-boundary review; no longer blocks hardening epic

title: Demos SDK shared UnifiedCrypto singleton causes multi-instance wallet identity bleed
description:
## Summary
Concurrent multi-wallet usage of the Demos SDK in a single process can collapse multiple `Demos` instances onto the same ed25519 identity. This corrupts signer-local state and produces malformed concurrent traffic in higher-level tests such as `gcr_identity_loadgen`.

## Deterministic Repro
Command:
`bun run repro:demosdk-multi-instance`

Inputs:
- `devnet/identities/node1.identity`
- `devnet/identities/node2.identity`
- default RPC `http://localhost:53554`

Observed on 2026-03-08:
- sequential phase stays distinct
  - instance A: `0x8db33f19486774dea73efbfed1175fb25fcf5a2682e1f55271207dc01670bb19`
  - instance B: `0x7bee59666b7ef18f648df18c4ed3677a79b30aaa6cf66dc6ab2818fd4be2dcfb`
- concurrent reconnect phase collapses both instances onto wallet B
  - instance A: `0x7bee59666b7ef18f648df18c4ed3677a79b30aaa6cf66dc6ab2818fd4be2dcfb`
  - instance B: `0x7bee59666b7ef18f648df18c4ed3677a79b30aaa6cf66dc6ab2818fd4be2dcfb`
- repro exits non-zero with:
  - `concurrent: both Demos instances resolved to the same identity 0x7bee...`

Additional production-facing evidence:
- `better_testing/runs/gcr-identity-loadgen-local-20260308-preflight-02/features/gcr/gcr_identity_loadgen.summary.json`
- node logs during concurrent GCR execution showed mixed sender fields in one transaction request, e.g. `content.from` from one wallet and `from_ed25519_address` from another.
- `better_testing/runs/consensus-rollback-local-20260308-04/features/consensus/consensus_rollback_smoke.summary.json` looked like a consensus tamper-acceptance bug at first, but node logs showed the execute request itself was sent and validated as `0x7bee...`, proving the scenario was polluted by SDK signer bleed rather than a clean node-level post-sign mutation acceptance repro.

## Root Cause Evidence
Installed SDK build shows process-global crypto state:
- `node_modules/@kynesyslabs/demosdk/build/websdk/demosclass.js:140`
  - `this.crypto = UnifiedCrypto.getInstance();`
- `node_modules/@kynesyslabs/demosdk/build/encryption/unifiedCrypto.js:76`
  - `static getInstance(instanceId, masterSeed)`
- `node_modules/@kynesyslabs/demosdk/build/encryption/unifiedCrypto.js:77-78`
  - no instance id passed => default singleton instance is reused

This means separate `new Demos()` objects can still share the same underlying `UnifiedCrypto` state unless the SDK explicitly scopes instances.

## Why It Matters
This is not just a loadgen artifact. Any real service that keeps more than one wallet/session alive in one process can leak signer state across logical clients. The node correctly rejects malformed mixed-signer transactions in some paths, but the SDK is still emitting invalid requests under realistic concurrent usage and can also create false-positive bug reports against node validation paths.

## Future Implementation Plan
1. Change the SDK so each `Demos` instance gets isolated crypto/wallet state instead of the implicit default `UnifiedCrypto` singleton.
2. Audit wallet connection, identity lookup, tx signing, and any helper paths that read/write signer state to ensure they are instance-local.
3. Add regression coverage that creates two `Demos` instances in one process with two different mnemonics and proves identities remain distinct before and after concurrent reconnect/sign flows.
4. Revalidate `gcr_identity_loadgen` with `CONCURRENCY=2` after the SDK fix.
5. Revalidate `consensus_rollback_smoke` after the SDK fix before asserting any node-side tamper-acceptance issue.
6. Only then revisit remaining concurrent transport issues such as `node-3qp` if they still reproduce.

## Current Status
Investigation is complete. Do not implement the SDK fix under this issue right now; use this bug as the future execution entry point.
acceptance_criteria:
1. Two `Demos` instances in one process, connected with two different mnemonics, always return distinct ed25519 identities.
2. Concurrent `connectWallet()` and signing on one instance do not mutate another instance's public key, private key, or tx sender fields.
3. `bun run repro:demosdk-multi-instance` exits zero after the fix and shows distinct identities in both sequential and concurrent phases.
4. `gcr_identity_loadgen` completes with `CONCURRENCY=2` without mixed-signer artifacts attributable to shared SDK state.
5. `consensus_rollback_smoke` no longer shows signer drift attributable to shared SDK state, allowing a clean reassessment of any node-side tamper handling.
notes: Standalone repro script: scripts/repro-demosdk-multi-instance-identity-bleed.ts. Investigation complete; implementation deferred.

comments.author: tcsenpai
comments.text:
Fresh confirmation on the stabilized devnet: the SDK signer-bleed symptom is still present inside the concurrent GCR run and remains distinct from the rate-limit bug.

Artifact:
- `better_testing/runs/validate-gcr-concurrent-20260309-01/features/gcr/gcr_identity_loadgen.summary.json`

Observed in `errorSamples[0]`:
- validated transaction content contains mixed sender state
- `content.from = 0x7bee...`
- `from_ed25519_address = 0x8db3...`
- validator message: `[SIGNATURE ERROR] Transaction signer does not match sender address`

This confirms the SDK multi-instance identity bleed bug still contaminates concurrent higher-level tests on the rebuilt cluster.

title: Create consensus behavior test scenarios
description:
## Problem
No automated testing of PoRBFT consensus behavior. Consensus bugs are the hardest to debug and most impactful.

## Scenarios to create
```
features/consensus/
  consensus_block_production.ts  - Verify blocks are produced at expected rate
  consensus_tx_inclusion.ts      - Submit tx, verify it appears in a block
  consensus_rollback_smoke.ts    - Trigger rollback scenario, verify state consistency
  consensus_secretary_rotation.ts - Verify secretary changes work
  consensus_partition_recovery.ts - Simulate partition, verify recovery
```

## Key validations
- Block height advances monotonically across all nodes
- Transaction finality within expected time window
- State consistency after rollbacks (no lost txs, no duplicates)
- Secretary manager correctly handles rotation/timeout

## Dependencies
- framework/ module extracted (P0 task)
- Devnet with 3+ nodes running

## Why
Consensus is the core of the network. A consensus bug that slips through can cause chain splits or data loss.
close_reason: Consensus scenario set implemented; remaining gaps tracked separately

title: Create ZK proof verification test scenarios
description:
## Problem
ZK identity system has no automated integration testing. Proof generation and verification paths are only tested via manual ceremony.

## Scenarios to create
```
features/zk/
  zk_commitment_smoke.ts       - Submit identity commitment, verify storage
  zk_attestation_smoke.ts      - Generate and verify ZK attestation
  zk_merkle_inclusion.ts       - Verify Merkle tree inclusion proof
  zk_proof_loadgen.ts          - Concurrent proof verifications
```

## Dependencies
- framework/ module extracted (P0 task)
- ZK trusted setup artifacts available in devnet
- snarkjs available in test container

## Why
ZK system is security-critical. Automated testing prevents regression in proof verification.
close_reason: Implemented ZK scenario batch; remaining failures are tracked as node bugs node-22q, node-324, and node-3vr

title: sync-br-to-myc leaves stale Mycelium epic when br issue changes type
description:
## Summary
The `sync-br-to-myc` bridge does not reconcile issue type changes from `br` when an issue previously imported as a Mycelium epic is later changed to a non-epic type.

## Repro
1. Start with a `br` issue imported into Mycelium as an epic.
2. Change that same `br` issue type from `epic` to `bug`.
3. Run `bun run sync:br-myc`.

## Observed on 2026-03-08
- `br` now reports `node-16j` as `bug`
- sync summary reports `br_epics: 4` but `myc_epics: 5`
- `myc epic list` still shows the stale epic:
  - `ID 6 | Demos SDK reuses ed25519 wallet identity across multiple instances in one process`
- `myc task list` now also shows the bug/task form:
  - `ID 32 | Demos SDK shared UnifiedCrypto singleton causes multi-instance wallet identity bleed`

## Impact
Tracker state drifts after legitimate `br` type mutations. This weakens Mycelium as a faithful mirror and creates duplicate work items.

## Likely Fix Area
- `scripts/sync-br-to-myc.ts`
- `.mycelium/br-sync-map.json`

## Suggested Fix Direction
When a mapped `br` issue changes type class between epic and non-epic, the sync should reconcile the prior Mycelium object instead of creating a second one. If Mycelium cannot mutate type in place, the sync should either archive/close the old object and remap, or record a deterministic migration step.
close_reason: sync-br-to-myc now reconciles epic/task type changes by deleting stale Mycelium objects and clearing old mappings

title: Consensus accepts tampered signed transaction after post-sign mutation
description:
## Reassessment
This item no longer stands as a confirmed node consensus bug.

## Fresh Repro
- Scenario: `better_testing/loadgen/src/features/consensus/consensus_rollback_smoke.ts`
- Run: `better_testing/scripts/run-scenario.sh consensus_rollback_smoke --run-id consensus-rollback-local-20260308-04`
- Artifact: `better_testing/runs/consensus-rollback-local-20260308-04/features/consensus/consensus_rollback_smoke.summary.json`

## What the artifact showed
- `confirmResult = 200`
- `broadcastResult = 200`
- stored tx sender fields all resolved to `0x7bee...`
- original intended sender nonce `0x8db3...` did not change

## Why this is not clean evidence of a node bug
Node logs for the same run show the execute request itself was authenticated and processed as `0x7bee...`, not `0x8db3...`:
- transport layer logged `Valid signature for: ed25519:0x7bee...`
- confirm path saw `content.from = 0x7bee...`
- `sender` inside the execute bundle was `null`

That means the rollback smoke was polluted by the already-known SDK multi-instance signer bleed (`node-16j`). The scenario creates two `Demos` instances in one process (`demos` and `recipientDemos`), and the current SDK can collapse signer state onto the second wallet. In this run, the supposedly tampered tx was not a trustworthy post-sign mutation repro; it was a transaction emitted under the wrong wallet identity before the node ever validated it.

## Conclusion
Close this as invalid / superseded by `node-16j` evidence pollution.

## Future follow-up
After `node-16j` is fixed, rerun `consensus_rollback_smoke` with an isolated signer model before reopening any consensus-level tamper-acceptance claim.
close_reason: Fresh repro was contaminated by Demos SDK multi-instance identity bleed; not reliable evidence of a node consensus validation bug

title: Fix async interval patterns - add error handlers to setInterval callbacks
notes: L2PS services already use safe* wrapper methods (safeGenerateAndRelayHashes, safeAggregateAndSubmit). No changes needed - these are already resilient.
close_reason: Already implemented - L2PS uses safeGenerateAndRelayHashes and safeAggregateAndSubmit wrappers

title: Avoid crashes
BunServer does not match GET routes with path params and CORS middleware rewrites 404 to 200
description:
## Summary
This is broader than a ZK nullifier endpoint bug. The custom `BunServer` only does exact path lookup, so GET routes declared with path params like `:hash` or `:commitment` never match. Then the CORS middleware rebuilds the response without preserving the original status, turning the fallback `404 Not Found` into `200 OK` with plain text body `Not Found` and `Content-Type: application/json`.

## Fresh Repro
Commands observed on 2026-03-08:
- `curl -i http://localhost:53551/zk/nullifier/0x000000000000000000000000000000000000000000aea19ccd44a84d40ce5a24`
- `curl -i http://localhost:53551/zk/nullifier/not-a-hash`
- `curl -i http://localhost:53551/zk/merkle/proof/0x4b3c2f7aab7fdd6c3a2e158a4ac3b2a7a8d7f1e9b8c6d4e2f0a1b2c3d4e5f607`

Observed for all of them:
- status: `200 OK`
- content-type: `application/json`
- body: `Not Found`

## Root Cause Evidence
1. `src/libs/network/server_rpc.ts` registers dynamic GET routes:
   - `/zk/merkle/proof/:commitment`
   - `/zk/nullifier/:hash`
2. `src/libs/network/bunServer.ts` route dispatch uses exact string lookup:
   - `const routeHandler = this.routes.get(method)?.get(path)`
   - this cannot match runtime paths against registered `:param` patterns
3. `src/libs/network/bunServer.ts` fallback returns `new Response("Not Found", { status: 404 })`
4. The `cors()` middleware rebuilds the response with only headers:
   - `return new Response(response.body, { headers })`
   - this drops the original status code, so the fallback 404 becomes a 200
5. The `json()` middleware then sets `Content-Type: application/json` even for the plain text fallback body

## User-Facing Impact
- ZK HTTP routes with path params are effectively unusable.
- Clients cannot distinguish route miss, invalid input, and legitimate JSON responses.
- `zk_attestation_smoke` parses nullifier checks as `null` because the body is not JSON.
- Any future GET route using `:param` will fail the same way.

## Affected Areas
- `src/libs/network/bunServer.ts`
- dynamic GET routes in `src/libs/network/server_rpc.ts`
- at minimum the currently observed ZK routes:
  - `/zk/nullifier/:hash`
  - `/zk/merkle/proof/:commitment`

## Suggested Fix Direction
1. Add path-parameter route matching to `BunServer` instead of exact-path-only lookup.
2. Preserve `status` and existing headers in `cors()` when rebuilding the response.
3. Keep `json()` from mislabeling non-JSON fallback bodies, or ensure all fallback/error bodies are valid JSON.
4. Add HTTP integration tests for dynamic GET route matching and status preservation.
5. Revalidate ZK smoke coverage after the server-layer fix, not by patching route handlers individually.

## Acceptance Criteria
1. `GET` routes registered with `:param` patterns are correctly matched at runtime.
2. Unknown routes remain `404`, not rewritten to `200`.
3. Dynamic ZK GET routes return their intended JSON responses.
4. Invalid nullifier hash input returns structured JSON with a non-2xx status.
5. `zk_attestation_smoke` can parse nullifier endpoint responses without endpoint-specific workarounds.
close_reason: Fixed by BunServer structured 404 work committed as 8389eb11; no longer open

comments.author: tcsenpai
comments.text:
Fresh revalidation on the stabilized devnet shows the dynamic GET routing bug still reproduces on all 4 healthy nodes.

Direct repro:
- `curl -sS -o /tmp/zk.body -w "status=%{http_code} content_type=%{content_type}" http://localhost:53551/zk/nullifier/0xdeadbeef`
- repeated on `53552`, `53553`, `53554`

Observed on all 4 nodes:
- HTTP status `200`
- content type `application/json`
- body literal `Not Found`

Additional scenario evidence:
- `better_testing/runs/validate-zk-attestation-20260309-01/features/zk/zk_attestation_smoke.summary.json`
- the attestation smoke still fails because before/after nullifier status checks return `status=200`, `ok=true`, `json=null` instead of a structured not-found / nullifier state response

This confirms the bug persists after the devnet cold-start fixes and is not caused by cluster instability.

title: Make POST_RUN_SETTLE_STRICT=true the default
close_reason: POST_RUN_SETTLE_STRICT now defaults to true in all token loadgen variants that perform post-run settle checks

title: Execute confirm can fail with signer mismatch under concurrent multi-wallet load
description: During gcr_identity_loadgen (CONCURRENCY=2 on localhost), node returned validation failures: '[SIGNATURE ERROR] Transaction signer does not match sender address'. This appeared when multiple Demos wallet sessions were active concurrently against the same node. Needs investigation in auth/session routing between execute sender identity and tx.from fields.
close_reason: Root cause isolated to shared Demos SDK wallet state across instances; node signer mismatch checks should remain in place

comments.author: tcsenpai
comments.text: Revalidation on 2026-03-08 after the localhost whitelist fix and devnet rebuild indicates node-2r7 is no longer the active blocker. Evidence: better_testing/runs/gcr-identity-loadgen-local-20260308-06/features/gcr/gcr_identity_loadgen.summary.json completed successfully with counters.rateLimited=0, no IP block behavior, and ok=true on the rebuilt cluster. A follow-up rerun in the more relevant concurrent shape (TARGETS=http://localhost:53554, CONCURRENCY=2, DURATION_SEC=8, MIN_LOOP_DELAY_MS=100, bounded by timeout 120s) did not emit Rate limit exceeded or IP blocked responses; instead the process overran the intended duration and was terminated by the outer timeout before writing a summary artifact. This means the remaining concurrent GCR problem has shifted away from HTTP/IP rate limiting and should be investigated under concurrent execute/confirm/broadcast behavior instead. Likely fix areas remain the execute/session/auth path and transaction signer derivation path. Acceptance for this issue should be: 1) gcr_identity_loadgen completes under CONCURRENCY=2 on the rebuilt local devnet without hanging past the configured duration plus settle window, 2) no signer mismatch validation errors occur under concurrent multi-wallet execution, 3) the scenario writes summary/timeseries artifacts reliably, and 4) no identities leak after cleanup.

comments.author: tcsenpai
comments.text:
Fresh repro on rebuilt devnet:
- `waitForTxReady(http://localhost:53554, 5)` fails because `nodeCall("crypto.getIdentity")` returns `{ error: "Unknown message"}`
- direct SDK path still works: `demos.connect`, `connectWallet`, and `demos.crypto.getIdentity("ed25519")` succeed
Impact:
- `gcr_identity_loadgen` can stall for the full startup timeout before artifacts are initialized, masking the real concurrent behavior
Likely fix area:
- loadgen harness readiness probe in `better_testing/loadgen/src/token_shared.ts`, not the node itself
Next action:
- replace the obsolete `crypto.getIdentity` nodeCall probe with a stable RPC/chain readiness probe, then rerun concurrent GCR validation

comments.author: tcsenpai
comments.text:
Validated on bounded rerun after harness fix:
- artifact: `better_testing/runs/gcr-identity-loadgen-local-20260308-debug-05/features/gcr/gcr_identity_loadgen.summary.json`
- `confirmTx` no longer shows the old signer-mismatch validation error
- `broadcastTx(add)` still fails with `Error: Tx sender and request sender address do not match. Transaction not applied.`
Root cause path:
- `src/libs/network/routines/transactions/handleIdentityRequest.ts` hard-rejects when `tx.content.from_ed25519_address !== sender`
- this still trips during broadcast under concurrent GCR identity assignment
Why it matters:
- concurrent identity assign/remove load cannot pass even after confirm-path hardening
Suggested fix direction:
- align broadcast identity sender validation with the signed transaction semantics, or ensure the request auth sender is guaranteed to match the tx ed25519 owner on relayed/concurrent execute flows
Acceptance criteria:
- `SCENARIO=gcr_identity_loadgen TARGETS=http://localhost:53554 CONCURRENCY=2 DURATION_SEC=8` completes without broadcast sender-mismatch errors
- no `Tx sender and request sender address do not match` in the run summary or node logs
- summary `errorRate` stays under threshold and no leaked identities remain

title: Genesis startup writes invalid transaction row on cold boot
description:
## Summary
On a fresh devnet volume, cold startup could crash during genesis persistence because the genesis transaction path wrote an incomplete `transactions` row.

## Observed Repro
- `docker compose down -v`
- `docker compose up -d --build --force-recreate`
- `node-2` restart-looped during startup
- node log showed:
  - `Failed to insert transaction ... null value in column "from_ed25519_address" of relation "transactions" violates not-null constraint`
  - followed by `current transaction is aborted, commands ignored until end of transaction block`

## Root Cause
Two code paths combined badly:
1. `src/libs/blockchain/chain.ts` built the genesis transaction without setting `content.from_ed25519_address`.
2. `src/libs/blockchain/routines/subOperations.ts` manually saved a `Transactions` entity for the genesis tx without populating required columns like `from`, `to`, `from_ed25519_address`, and fee fields.

## Fix Applied
- set genesis sender sentinel to `0x0` at genesis transaction creation
- persist all required transaction entity fields when saving the genesis transaction record

## Validation
After the fix:
- `docker compose up -d --build --force-recreate` starts all 4 nodes successfully from a clean volume
- `node-2` no longer restart-loops on genesis insertion
- representative scenarios pass on the rebuilt devnet

## Acceptance Criteria
1. Fresh-volume devnet boot does not fail on `transactions` table constraints.
2. All nodes can persist and query the genesis transaction consistently.
3. `docker compose ps` stays healthy across all four nodes after cold start.

title: ZK commitment assignment is shaped into remove/null GCR edit during validation
description:
## Summary
ZK identity assignment transactions are still being shaped into the wrong GCR mutation. Fresh `zk_commitment_smoke` on the stabilized devnet shows `zk_commitmentadd` validates successfully, but the resulting `gcr_edits` entry is malformed: it is emitted as `operation: "remove"` with `data: null` under `context: "zk"`.

## Fresh Repro
Command:
- `better_testing/scripts/run-scenario.sh zk_commitment_smoke --run-id validate-zk-commitment-20260309-01`

Artifact:
- `better_testing/runs/validate-zk-commitment-20260309-01/features/zk/zk_commitment_smoke.summary.json`

## Observed Behavior
- transaction validity result is `200` and `valid: true`
- input payload requests:
  - `method: "zk_commitmentadd"`
  - context `zk`
  - payload contains the expected `commitment_hash`, provider, and timestamp
- but the shaped identity edit in the validated transaction is:
  - `operation: "remove"`
  - `context: "zk"`
  - `data: null`
- `malformedGcrEditDetected = true`
- broadcast is not attempted because the scenario correctly stops once the malformed edit is detected

## Why It Matters
- ZK commitment assignment cannot be trusted end-to-end even before broadcast.
- The validation/shaping layer is corrupting semantic intent before the transaction ever reaches consensus.
- This blocks meaningful ZK commitment smoke coverage and can mask deeper node behavior because the tx is already wrong pre-broadcast.

## Likely Fix Areas
- ZK identity / commitment transaction shaping logic before `gcr_edits` are finalized
- any handler that maps `zk_commitmentadd` or `zk_attestationadd` into GCR identity edits
- validation path that rewrites intent into `operation: remove` with null data

## Acceptance Criteria
1. `zk_commitmentadd` produces a shaped GCR edit that preserves add semantics.
2. The shaped edit contains non-null commitment payload data.
3. `zk_commitment_smoke` proceeds beyond validation on a healthy devnet without `malformedGcrEditDetected`.
close_reason: Resolved during production-ready test-suite stabilization and closed in the active Mycelium tracker

title: Fresh devnet bootstrap fails because genesis transaction violates transactions.from_ed25519_address NOT NULL
description:
## Summary
On a fresh devnet bootstrap against an empty Postgres volume, node startup fails during genesis insertion because the genesis transaction is persisted with `from_ed25519_address = null/undefined`, but the `transactions` table declares that column as NOT NULL.

## Clean Repro
1. Reset the devnet state:
   - `docker compose -f devnet/docker-compose.yml down -v`
2. Start a fresh stack:
   - `docker compose -f devnet/docker-compose.yml up -d postgres node-1 node-2 node-3 node-4`
3. Inspect node logs:
   - `docker logs demos-devnet-node-1 --tail 60`

## Observed Behavior
Fresh startup reaches genesis processing and then fails with:
- `Failed to insert transaction ... null value in column "from_ed25519_address" of relation "transactions" violates not-null constraint`
- followed by:
  - `Failed to insert block 0 ... current transaction is aborted, commands ignored until end of transaction block`
- `main()` then exits and the node re-enters the compose restart loop.

Representative log excerpt from 2026-03-09:
- `toRawTransaction - Creating raw tx: ... type=genesis`
- `Failed to insert transaction ... null value in column "from_ed25519_address" ...`
- `[FATAL] Error in main() ... current transaction is aborted, commands ignored until end of transaction block`

## Root Cause Evidence
The code paths line up directly:
- `src/libs/blockchain/chain.ts`
  - `generateGenesisBlock()` creates `genesisTx`
  - sets `genesisTx.content.type`, `from`, `to`, fees, nonce, timestamp
  - does **not** set `genesisTx.content.from_ed25519_address`
- `src/libs/blockchain/transaction.ts`
  - `toRawTransaction()` copies `tx.content.from_ed25519_address` directly into the raw DB row
- `src/model/entities/Transactions.ts`
  - `from_ed25519_address` is a non-null `@Column("varchar")`

So a clean bootstrap on an empty DB deterministically generates a raw transaction that violates the schema.

## Why It Matters
- Prevents clean devnet bootstrap from empty state.
- Blocks repeatable fresh-environment testing, which is required for the universal test suite.
- Produces restart-loop behavior even though the real bug is a deterministic genesis persistence mismatch.

## Likely Fix Areas
- `src/libs/blockchain/chain.ts`
  - genesis transaction shaping in `generateGenesisBlock()`
- `src/libs/blockchain/transaction.ts`
  - raw transaction serialization for special transaction types like `genesis`
- `src/model/entities/Transactions.ts`
  - schema expectations for genesis rows, if null sender is intentional

## Suggested Fix Direction
1. Decide the contract for genesis transactions in the relational `transactions` table.
2. Either:
   - populate `from_ed25519_address` with a deterministic synthetic value for genesis, or
   - make the column nullable for genesis-compatible persistence and handle that case explicitly.
3. Keep block insertion from entering an aborted SQL transaction because one genesis tx row violates schema.
4. Add a fresh-bootstrap regression check that starts from empty Postgres state and verifies all four nodes come up.

## Acceptance Criteria
1. `docker compose -f devnet/docker-compose.yml down -v && docker compose -f devnet/docker-compose.yml up -d postgres node-1 node-2 node-3 node-4` succeeds on a clean state.
2. Node logs no longer show `from_ed25519_address` NOT NULL violations during genesis insertion.
3. The genesis block and genesis transaction are both persisted in a schema-compatible way.
4. All four local RPC nodes answer `/ping` after fresh bootstrap.
close_reason: Resolved by the genesis cold-start fix captured in node-2a2.

title: EVM XM identity verification compares targetAddress case-sensitively
description:
## Summary
XM identity verification for EVM payloads currently compares the recovered signer address to `targetAddress` with raw string equality. This rejects otherwise valid Ethereum addresses when the payload uses a different letter casing than the recovered EIP-55 checksum form.

## Repro
1. Run `better_testing/scripts/run-scenario.sh gcr_identity_xm_smoke --build --run-id gcr-identity-xm-local-20260308-01` with a lowercase `targetAddress` recipe.
2. Submit an `xm_identity_assign` payload where:
   - `chain = "evm"`
   - `subchain = "mainnet"`
   - `chainId = 1`
   - `signedData = <owner ed25519 address>`
   - `signature = await ethersWallet.signMessage(signedData)`
   - `targetAddress = ethersWallet.address.toLowerCase()`
3. Observe confirm succeeds but broadcast rejects with `evm payload signature could not be verified. Transaction not applied.`

Observed on 2026-03-08:
- Lowercased address form fails.
- The same payload succeeds when `targetAddress` uses the wallet's canonical checksummed `ethersWallet.address` string.
- Successful control run artifact: `better_testing/runs/gcr-identity-xm-local-20260308-02/features/gcr/gcr_identity_xm_smoke.summary.json`

## Why It Matters
Ethereum addresses are effectively case-insensitive for identity purposes, with checksum casing as an encoding aid. Raw case-sensitive comparison in node verification makes the XM payload contract unnecessarily brittle for SDKs, scripts, and external clients.

## Likely Fix Area
- `src/libs/blockchain/gcr/gcr_routines/identityManager.ts`
  - `verifyPayload()` EVM path

## Suggested Fix Direction
1. Normalize both the recovered EVM address and `targetAddress` before comparison.
2. Prefer checksum-aware normalization if available; otherwise lowercase both safely.
3. Add tests for checksummed and lowercase payload addresses.
4. Keep stored display casing explicit while normalizing comparisons.

## Acceptance Criteria
1. A valid EVM XM payload verifies successfully regardless of checksum-vs-lowercase casing in `targetAddress`.
2. Invalid signatures still fail.
3. `gcr_identity_xm_smoke` passes when forced to use lowercase `targetAddress` in the payload.
4. No regression for XM identity add/remove persistence or duplicate detection.
close_reason: Normalized EVM XM address verification in IdentityManager and validated lowercase targetAddress add/remove flow on all 4 nodes

title: Peer bootstrap exits on transient genesis-hash startup race
description:
## Summary
Peer bootstrap could exit the whole node during cold start if a healthy peer had not yet materialized its genesis block for `getGenesisDataHash`.

## Observed Repro
After fixing the invalid genesis transaction write, a fresh devnet boot still showed `node-2` exiting during startup:
- bootstrap contacted `node-1`
- `node-1` returned `500 INTERNAL_ERROR` for `nodeCall(getGenesisDataHash)` during its own startup window
- `src/libs/peer/routines/peerBootstrap.ts` treated any non-200 response as fatal and called `process.exit(1)`

## Root Cause
- `src/libs/network/manageNodeCall.ts` returns `500` if `Chain.getGenesisBlock()` is not ready yet.
- `src/libs/peer/routines/peerBootstrap.ts` used `getGenesisDataHash` as a one-shot fatal check instead of a retryable readiness probe.
- On staggered cold boot, that made peer bootstrap brittle even though the cluster became healthy seconds later.

## Fix Applied
- added bounded retry logic around `getGenesisDataHash` in peer bootstrap
- preserved fatal behavior only after repeated failures, with response details logged

## Validation
After the fix:
- fresh `docker compose up -d --build --force-recreate` leaves all 4 nodes up
- all four nodes return the same genesis hash over RPC
- representative consensus, Omni, and GCR scenarios pass on the rebuilt devnet

## Acceptance Criteria
1. A cold start with staggered peer readiness does not kill nodes during bootstrap.
2. Transient `getGenesisDataHash` unavailability is retried instead of causing immediate process exit.
3. All nodes converge and stay up after fresh devnet boot.

title: Replace silent .catch(() => {}) with logging
notes: Fixed 2 silent catches in L2PSConcurrentSync.ts - now log debug messages. Remaining silent catches are intentional (gracefulShutdown).
close_reason: Fixed silent catches in L2PSConcurrentSync.ts with debug logging

title: HTTP rate limiter localhost whitelist is ineffective in local devnet
description:
## Problem
The HTTP rate limiter is intended to always whitelist localhost traffic, but local devnet nodes still return `IP blocked due to rate limiting` for loopback requests. The root cause appears to be IPv6 loopback addresses not being included in the HTTP whitelist path.

## Evidence
1. `src/utilities/sharedState.ts` default `whitelistedIPs` only included `127.0.0.1`
2. `src/libs/network/middleware/rateLimiter.ts` checks `config.whitelistedIPs.includes(clientIP)` before block logic
3. `getClientIP()` can return IPv6 loopback values from `server.requestIP(req)` such as `::1` or `::ffff:127.0.0.1`
4. observed behavior on local devnet before patching config defaults: `http://localhost:53551`, `53552`, and `53553` were blocked while `53554` stayed healthy

## Why It Matters
This breaks multi-node automated testing on local devnet even when the code comment says localhost should always be whitelisted. It also makes rate-limit behavior depend on IPv4 versus IPv6 resolution rather than explicit policy.

## Likely Fix Areas
1. `src/utilities/sharedState.ts` rateLimitConfig.whitelistedIPs
2. `src/libs/network/middleware/rateLimiter.ts` local loopback handling and IP normalization

## Suggested Fix Strategy
1. Treat `127.0.0.1`, `::1`, and `::ffff:127.0.0.1` as equivalent localhost loopback addresses.
2. Normalize loopback forms before whitelist lookup to avoid policy drift between IPv4 and IPv6.
3. Restart local devnet and verify blocked loopback IPs are bypassed immediately without manual unblock.
4. Add a small unit or integration test around `getClientIP()` and whitelist matching if feasible.
acceptance_criteria:
1. Requests from IPv4 and IPv6 localhost both bypass HTTP rate limiting by default.
2. Local devnet nodes no longer return `IP blocked due to rate limiting` for loopback traffic under the same test load.
3. Whitelist behavior is consistent for `127.0.0.1`, `::1`, and `::ffff:127.0.0.1`.
4. Existing non-local rate limiting behavior remains unchanged.
close_reason: Validated localhost IPv4/IPv6 whitelist fix after rebuilt devnet

title: Extract framework/ shared module from duplicated code
description:
## Problem
envInt(), envBool(), sleep(), percentile(), normalizeHexAddress(), fetchTokenSnapshot() are copy-pasted across 22+ test files. Any change requires editing dozens of files.

## Fix
Create better_testing/loadgen/src/framework/:

```
framework/
  common.ts     <- sleep, envInt, envBool, splitCsv, nowMs, percentile
  rpc.ts        <- rpcPost, nodeCall (parameterized message mapping)
  consistency.ts <- generic cross-node polling + snapshot comparison
  metrics.ts    <- ReservoirSampler, Counters type, timeseries helpers
  io.ts         <- from run_io.ts (unchanged)
  scenario.ts   <- registerScenario() replaces switch statement
```

## Steps
1. Create framework/common.ts with all duplicated helpers
2. Create framework/metrics.ts with ReservoirSampler + percentile + Counters type
3. Move run_io.ts -> framework/io.ts
4. Create framework/rpc.ts extracting rpcPost/nodeCall from token_shared.ts
5. Create framework/consistency.ts with generic cross-node polling
6. Update all 22+ files to import from framework/

## Evidence of duplication
Files reimplementing envInt/envBool: token_smoke.ts, token_transfer_loadgen.ts, token_mint_loadgen.ts, token_burn_loadgen.ts, rpc_loadgen.ts, im_online_loadgen.ts, token_acl_smoke.ts, token_acl_matrix.ts, token_consensus_consistency.ts, token_edge_cases.ts, token_query_coverage.ts, token_pause_under_load.ts, + 10 more

## Why
Unblocks ALL other testing tasks. Reduces maintenance burden by ~80%.
close_reason: Completed framework extraction, scenario registry dispatch, and rpc helper parameterization for universal loadgen suite

title: Concurrent GCR execute path can still hit request rate limiting after localhost whitelist fix
close_reason: Revalidated after fix a4b04a73: concurrent gcr_identity_loadgen no longer reproduces Rate limit exceeded or IP blocked transport failures; remaining failures are SDK signer-bleed validation errors.

comments.author: tcsenpai
comments.text:
Repro:
- `SCENARIO=gcr_identity_loadgen TARGETS=http://localhost:53554 CONCURRENCY=2 DURATION_SEC=8 MIN_LOOP_DELAY_MS=100 SETTLE_TIMEOUT_SEC=8 SETTLE_POLL_MS=200 GCR_OP_TIMEOUT_MS=10000 bun better_testing/loadgen/src/main.ts`
Observed:
- run artifact `better_testing/runs/gcr-identity-loadgen-local-20260308-debug-05/features/gcr/gcr_identity_loadgen.summary.json` shows `confirm transport failed (add): {"error":"Rate limit exceeded"}` even after localhost whitelist validation
- same run has `rateLimited: 1`
Why it matters:
- concurrent GCR load still trips request throttling in the execute path on local test infra, so it cannot serve as a stable universal-suite signal
Likely fix areas:
- request auth / rate-limiter interaction for execute requests
- whitelist logic coverage for the specific request path used by SDK `execute`
Suggested fix direction:
- trace how execute requests are classified by the rate limiter and ensure localhost/devnet traffic is exempt consistently
Acceptance criteria:
- rerunning the scenario above produces `rateLimited: 0`
- no `Rate limit exceeded` error samples in summary artifacts
- result remains bounded by real tx failures only

comments.author: tcsenpai
comments.text:
Fresh revalidation evidence on 2026-03-09:
- exact `node-uag` repro shape aimed at `http://localhost:53554` could not even start scenario execution because `waitForRpcReady()` never observed a healthy RPC for 120s
- command: `RUNS_DIR=better_testing/runs SCENARIO=gcr_identity_loadgen TARGETS=http://localhost:53554 CONCURRENCY=1 DURATION_SEC=6 MIN_LOOP_DELAY_MS=300 SETTLE_TIMEOUT_SEC=8 SETTLE_POLL_MS=250 GCR_OP_TIMEOUT_MS=15000 bun run better_testing/loadgen/src/main.ts`
- direct probe during that window returned `{"error":"IP blocked due to rate limiting","retryAfter":null}` from `GET /ping` on `53554`
- bounded run exited with: `RPC not ready at http://localhost:53554/ after 120s`
Why this matters:
- the active operational blocker is broader than just inner execute requests; on the current devnet a node can become unusable for test preflight itself because the HTTP readiness probe is rate-limited
- this prevented a same-target fresh `node-uag` confirmation even though `node-uag` was separately reproduced on `53553`
Acceptance addition:
- `GET /ping` on local devnet targets used by the universal suite must remain usable under normal test traffic and must not enter an IP-blocked state during single-worker serialized validation

comments.author: tcsenpai
comments.text:
Fresh concurrent repro on 2026-03-09 confirms this bug is still active on the rebuilt devnet.

Command:
- `timeout 180s env RUNS_DIR=better_testing/runs SCENARIO=gcr_identity_loadgen TARGETS=http://localhost:53553 CONCURRENCY=2 DURATION_SEC=6 MIN_LOOP_DELAY_MS=300 SETTLE_TIMEOUT_SEC=8 SETTLE_POLL_MS=250 MAX_ERROR_RATE=0.2 GCR_OP_TIMEOUT_MS=15000 bun better_testing/loadgen/src/main.ts`

Artifact:
- `better_testing/runs/2026-03-09T15_44_38.602Z/features/gcr/gcr_identity_loadgen.summary.json`

Observed in the same run:
- explicit transport error `{"error":"Rate limit exceeded"}`
- then repeated transport errors `{"error":"IP blocked due to rate limiting"}`
- `errorCount = 12`
- `rateLimited = 0` in counters, which means current summary bucketing is undercounting these transport-level throttle failures even though the raw samples clearly show them
- same run also contains SDK signer-bleed validation failures (`Transaction signer does not match sender address`), but the rate-limit evidence is independent because the raw confirm transport responses explicitly contain the throttle messages

Why this matters:
- even after the localhost whitelist fix, concurrent universal-suite traffic can still drive a healthy node into request throttling and temporary IP block state
- this means current GCR concurrency scenarios are still contaminated by infra throttling in addition to SDK-state issues

Acceptance addition:
- concurrent `gcr_identity_loadgen` at `CONCURRENCY=2` on a healthy local devnet must produce no `Rate limit exceeded` and no `IP blocked due to rate limiting` transport responses
- summary counters should also classify these throttle responses accurately instead of leaving `rateLimited = 0` when raw samples contain throttle messages

comments.author: tcsenpai
comments.text:
Fresh revalidation on the stabilized cold-boot-fixed devnet still reproduces the rate-limit blocker.

Repro:
- `better_testing/scripts/run-scenario.sh gcr_identity_loadgen --run-id validate-gcr-concurrent-20260309-01 --env TARGETS=http://node-1:53551,http://node-2:53552,http://node-3:53553,http://node-4:53554 --env CONCURRENCY=2 --env DURATION_SEC=8`

Artifact:
- `better_testing/runs/validate-gcr-concurrent-20260309-01/features/gcr/gcr_identity_loadgen.summary.json`

Observed:
- active transport failures include both `Rate limit exceeded` and repeated `IP blocked due to rate limiting` during `confirm`
- same run also shows SDK signer-bleed evidence, but the rate-limit symptom is independently present in raw samples
- examples from `errorSamples`:
  - `confirm transport failed (add)` with `{ "error": "Rate limit exceeded" }`
  - repeated `{ "error": "IP blocked due to rate limiting", "retryAfter": null }`

Important nuance:
- summary counters still show `rateLimited: 0`, so the run artifact undercounts throttle failures at the bucket/counter layer
- this does not invalidate the node bug; the raw samples clearly show the live rate-limit path is still active on the rebuilt devnet

Why this matters:
- concurrent GCR validation is still blocked even after the devnet cold-start fixes
- rate limiting is still aggressive enough to poison the validation path itself, not just high-throughput stress traffic

Acceptance remains the same:
- concurrent GCR runs should no longer return `Rate limit exceeded` or `IP blocked due to rate limiting` during the validation path on local test traffic

title: Devnet startup fails opaquely when postgres is absent from the compose network
description:
## Summary
This is not a generic permanent node crash. The immediate trigger observed on 2026-03-09 was that the devnet `postgres` service was absent/disconnected from the compose network, and all node containers then entered a restart loop with an opaque startup error:
- `[FATAL] Error in main() - attempting graceful shutdown { error: "getaddrinfo ENOTFOUND", stack: undefined }`

The operational problem is real, but the node-side bug is the poor diagnosability: startup does not identify the failing hostname or dependency, so the cluster looks like an unexplained node regression instead of a missing database service.

## Observed Repro
While attempting to revalidate `node-uag`:
- `docker compose -f devnet/docker-compose.yml ps` showed all node services restarting and no running `postgres` service
- `docker inspect demos-devnet-postgres --format '{{json .NetworkSettings.Networks}}'` showed no assigned IP/endpoint during the failure window
- node logs from `demos-devnet-node-1` and `demos-devnet-node-4` repeatedly showed only:
  - startup banner
  - `bun -r tsconfig-paths/register src/index.ts --no-tui`
  - `[FATAL] Error in main() - attempting graceful shutdown { error: "getaddrinfo ENOTFOUND", stack: undefined }`

Representative commands:
- `docker compose -f devnet/docker-compose.yml ps`
- `docker logs demos-devnet-node-1 --tail 120`
- `docker logs demos-devnet-node-4 --tail 120`
- `docker inspect demos-devnet-postgres --format '{{json .NetworkSettings.Networks}}'`

## Recovery Evidence
Running:
- `docker compose -f devnet/docker-compose.yml up -d postgres`

brought `demos-devnet-postgres` back onto `demos-devnet_demos-network`, after which all four nodes came back up and resumed normal peer/consensus activity.

That strongly narrows the failure path to startup code that needs `PG_HOST=postgres` early, with `Chain.setup() -> Datasource.getInstance() -> TypeORM DataSource.initialize()` being the strongest candidate path.

## Why It Matters
- Blocks live validation of test-suite issues such as `node-uag`.
- Produces a misleading node-level fatal instead of a specific infra/dependency diagnosis.
- Makes devnet recovery slower because logs do not tell the operator that the missing service is `postgres`.

## Likely Fix Areas
- `src/index.ts`
  - startup error reporting in `main().catch(...)`
- `src/model/datasource.ts`
  - database initialization path for `PG_HOST=postgres`
- devnet startup / health workflow
  - clearer dependency checks before node startup proceeds
- optional: peer/bootstrap path logging, though current evidence points more strongly to DB init than peer bootstrap

## Suggested Fix Direction
1. When startup fails, log the full error object, stack, and nested cause so the failing hostname/service is visible.
2. Add an explicit preflight for database readiness / hostname resolution before `Chain.setup()` proceeds.
3. If `postgres` is absent or unresolved, fail with a direct message such as `Postgres host postgres is not resolvable/reachable` instead of raw `ENOTFOUND`.
4. Consider making the devnet wrapper or compose workflow surface the missing `postgres` service before nodes begin restart-looping.

## Acceptance Criteria
1. If `postgres` is missing or unresolved, node startup logs clearly identify that dependency and the failing hostname.
2. On a healthy compose network, `docker compose -f devnet/docker-compose.yml ps` shows `postgres` plus all four nodes up.
3. Local devnet recovery no longer requires manual forensic inspection of generic `ENOTFOUND` logs.
4. Blocked scenario revalidations such as `node-uag` can resume once the service is restored.
close_reason: Stabilized devnet cold start; concrete boot blockers were resolved in node-2a2 and node-3is, and the earlier postgres-only diagnosis was superseded.

title: Active node test hardening
description:
## Summary
Drive the next node-local testing phase after the production-ready stabilization work.

## Scope
- close the remaining node-local testing gaps for active and implemented features only
- convert the active-feature coverage matrix and proposal into executable follow-up tasks
- improve startup, routine validation, and operational confidence without inventing work for inactive stubs

## Authoritative References
- `docs/references/active-feature-test-coverage-matrix.md`
- `docs/specs/active-feature-test-addition-proposal.md`

## Non-Goals
- no native bridge execution work
- no storageProgram runtime expansion beyond placeholder semantics
- no storage-contract / contract-runtime execution work
- no SDK-side wallet isolation fixes in this repo; those remain deferred and only noted here

title: Add cold-boot startup suite
description:
## Goal
Create a dedicated cold-boot validation suite for the local devnet.

## Why
- startup confidence is currently inferred from downstream suite success rather than proven directly

## Definition of Done
- start from a clean local devnet state
- verify health endpoints respond
- verify peer discovery converges
- verify block production starts
- record a run artifact in `better_testing/runs/`
- document the command in the better_testing docs

## Command Shape
`bun better_testing/scripts/run-suite.ts <startup-suite> --local`

title: Audit process.exit() calls - convert to graceful shutdown
description:
## Problem
50+ process.exit() calls scattered throughout the codebase. These bypass graceful shutdown, leaving connections open, DB transactions uncommitted, and peers unnotified.

## Evidence
| Area | File | Lines |
|------|------|-------|
| Main | src/index.ts | 131, 611, 705, 720, 880, 983, 987 |
| Consensus | PoRBFT.ts | 260, 388, 404, 612, 688, 808, 903, 920 |
| Consensus | secretaryManager.ts | multiple |
| Peer | peerBootstrap.ts | 80, 100, 110, 117, 173, 200 |
| Sync | Sync.ts | 257, 266, 274, 858 |
| TLSNotary | TLSNotaryService.ts | 342, 351, 373, 439, 458, 492 |

## Approach
For each process.exit(), decide:
1. **Is this truly fatal?** → Replace with gracefulShutdown()
2. **Is this recoverable?** → Log error, return, don't exit
3. **Is this startup validation?** → Keep exit but add clear message

## Code Snippet
```typescript
// Before - abrupt exit
if (someConditionFailed) {
  console.error('Something failed')
  process.exit(1)
}

// After - graceful shutdown
if (someConditionFailed) {
  logger.error('Something failed - initiating graceful shutdown')
  await gracefulShutdown('condition_failed')
  return
}

// Or for recoverable errors:
if (someConditionFailed) {
  logger.error('Something failed - skipping this operation')
  return { error: 'Condition failed' }
}
```

## Why This Helps Stability
- DB transactions commit or rollback cleanly
- Peers are notified before disconnect
- Resources are released properly
- Logs capture shutdown reason
- Monitoring can detect graceful vs crash exits
notes:
AUDIT COMPLETE. 75 process.exit() calls found:

KEEP (valid fatal errors):
- Startup/bootstrap: Genesis mismatch, config errors (peerBootstrap, Sync, index.ts)
- Graceful shutdown: SIGTERM/SIGINT handlers (index.ts:906,1009,1013)
- Consensus: Secretary timeout, block commit failures (secretaryManager.ts)

IGNORE (not runtime):
- Tests: test_*.ts, benchmark.ts
- Scripts: ceremony.ts, setup-zk.ts
- Documentation: README.md, GITBOOK_PAGE.md

OPTIONAL IMPROVEMENT (low priority):
- TLSNotary: Has fatal flag, could disable service instead of exit
- PeerManager: Line 61 - could retry instead of exit

RECOMMENDATION: Most exits are valid - they indicate unrecoverable state. Adding graceful shutdown for ALL would mask critical failures. The global error handlers (node-2um) already catch unexpected crashes.
close_reason: Audit complete - most exits are valid fatal errors. Global handlers (node-2um) catch unexpected crashes. Low-priority improvements documented.

title: TLSNotary service unhealthy on local prod-gate validation

Observed:

- all four nodes return /tlsnotary/info enabled=true, running=false
- all four nodes return /tlsnotary/health status=unhealthy with error="Docker container not accessible"

Artifact:

Why it matters:

- this is separate from the multichain execute-path promotion completed in node-7v8.3

title: HTTP rate limiter blocks multi-node consensus validation on local devnet
description: Consensus scenario validation cannot be executed across multiple HTTP RPC nodes on the local devnet because three of the four nodes return rate-limit block responses. During development on 2026-03-08, localhost:53554 stayed healthy while 53551, 53552, and 53553 returned IP blocked due to rate limiting. This prevents cross-node checks for block production, tx visibility, and future partition or recovery scenarios. Related issues: node-3mq for localhost whitelist failure and node-2r7 for loadgen rate-limit sensitivity. Suggested path: fix localhost whitelist behavior first, rerun consensus scenarios across all nodes, and if still needed add a deterministic dev/test-only HTTP rate-limit profile without weakening production defaults.
close_reason: Multi-node consensus validation restored on rebuilt local devnet

comments.author: tcsenpai
comments.text: Detailed context: on 2026-03-08 local consensus validation could only use http://localhost:53554 because 53551, 53552, and 53553 returned IP blocked due to rate limiting. This prevents multi-node checks for block production, tx visibility, and future partition/recovery scenarios. Related issues are node-3mq for localhost whitelist failure and node-2r7 for loadgen rate-limit sensitivity. Expected resolution path: fix localhost whitelist first, rerun consensus scenarios on all nodes, and only if still necessary add a deterministic dev/test-only HTTP rate-limit profile.

title: Parameterize nodeCall() message mapping for non-token features
description:
## Problem
nodeCall() in token_shared.ts has hardcoded fallback logic for token.* methods only (e.g. token.getBalance -> token.getBalanceCommitted). Non-token tests (GCR, consensus, peer sync) can't reuse this.

## Fix
Refactor nodeCall() to accept a message-to-committed mapping:
```typescript
// Before (hardcoded)
if (method === 'token.get') fallback = 'token.getCommitted'

// After (parameterized)
type CommittedFallbackMap = Record<string, string>
const TOKEN_FALLBACKS: CommittedFallbackMap = {
  'token.get': 'token.getCommitted',
  'token.getBalance': 'token.getBalanceCommitted',
}
function nodeCall(rpcUrl, message, data, fallbackMap?: CommittedFallbackMap)
```

## Why
Required before ANY non-token test can be written. Blocks GCR, consensus, and peer sync test tasks.
close_reason: framework/rpc nodeCall now accepts fallback maps; non-token scenario uses NO_FALLBACKS and compiles

title: Create peer sync test scenarios
description:
## Problem
No automated testing of peer discovery, bootstrap, or sync behavior. Sync bugs cause nodes to fall behind or crash (we found process.exit calls in Sync.ts during the crash audit).

## Scenarios to create
```
features/peersync/
  sync_catchup_smoke.ts       - New node syncs to current height
  sync_consistency.ts         - All nodes at same height after N blocks
  peer_discovery_smoke.ts     - Node discovers peers from peerlist
  sync_under_load.ts          - Sync while txs are being submitted
```

## Key validations
- Node reaches consensus height within timeout
- Block hashes match across all nodes at same height
- Peer count stabilizes after bootstrap
- Sync doesn't crash under concurrent tx load

## Dependencies
- framework/ module extracted (P0 task)

## Why
Peer sync issues cause node crashes (Sync.ts:257,266,274,858 all call process.exit). Testing prevents regressions.
close_reason: Peer/sync scenario batch implemented and validated on local devnet

title: Document and stabilize XM identity payload requirements for automated tests
description: XM identity automation needs a deterministic recipe for payload generation (required chainId, signature encoding, expected target_identity fields). Current loadgen attempts can fail at broadcast with signature format errors if payload is synthetic.
close_reason: Implemented deterministic EVM XM identity smoke recipe and validated add/remove convergence across all 4 devnet nodes

title: PQC identity removal can remain visible longer than loadgen settle window
description:
## Summary
This is now a confirmed active node-side convergence bug. Serialized single-worker GCR identity loadgen reproduced it again on 2026-03-09 on a healthy current devnet target, without concurrency, signer bleed, or rate limiting.

The failure mode is stable:
- add succeeds
- visibility settle for add succeeds
- remove succeeds far enough for the test to begin polling for disappearance
- DB-visible identity state still shows the PQC identity for more than 8 seconds
- final post-run check later shows the identity is eventually gone

So removal is eventually applied, but not within the current committed-state settle budget.

## Fresh Confirmed Repro
Artifact:
`better_testing/runs/2026-03-09T15_40_11.078Z/features/gcr/gcr_identity_loadgen.summary.json`

Command shape:
- `SCENARIO=gcr_identity_loadgen`
- `TARGETS=http://localhost:53553`
- `CONCURRENCY=1`
- `DURATION_SEC=6`
- `MIN_LOOP_DELAY_MS=300`
- `SETTLE_TIMEOUT_SEC=8`
- `SETTLE_POLL_MS=250`
- `GCR_OP_TIMEOUT_MS=15000`

Observed:
- `errorSamples[0].code = CONVERGENCE`
- `errorSamples[0].message = settle failed: identity still visible after 8s`
- `errorSamples[0].context.expectPresent = false`
- `errorSamples[0].context.algorithm = falcon`
- `rateLimited = 0`
- `finalChecks[0].stillPresent = []`
- `leakedIdentities = []`

That combination matters: the absence wait failed, but the final sweep was clean, so the identity disappeared eventually after the settle window had already been exceeded.

## Earlier Historical Repro
Artifact:
`better_testing/runs/gcr-identity-loadgen-local-20260308-05/features/gcr/gcr_identity_loadgen.summary.json`

Observed with the same serialized shape on `http://localhost:53554`.

## Why This Is Meaningful
Current loadgen flow in `better_testing/loadgen/src/features/gcr/gcr_identity_loadgen.ts` is:
1. submit add
2. wait until identity is visible
3. submit remove
4. wait until identity is absent

If remove confirm/broadcast had failed outright, the test would have failed before the second settle wait. Instead, the failure is specifically from the absence polling path after remove was accepted far enough for the test to monitor disappearance.

The observation path is DB-backed, not an in-memory illusion:
- loadgen polls `getAddressInfo`
- node handles that through `manageNodeCall -> ensureGCRForUser(data.address)`
- `ensureGCRForUser` reads the persisted `GCR_Main` row
- PQC identities come from the persisted `identities.pqc` state

## What Is Ruled Out In This Repro
- SDK multi-instance signer bleed (`node-16j`): not applicable, `CONCURRENCY=1`
- HTTP/IP rate limiting (`node-3qp`): `rateLimited = 0`
- leaked final state: final sweep is clean
- pure test harness startup failure: preflight passed and the scenario executed the full add/remove cycle

## Best Current Read
Most likely explanations are:
1. accepted PQC remove can lag in DB-visible GCR state for longer than 8 seconds
2. there is async post-processing between accepted remove and durable `GCR_Main.identities.pqc` convergence
3. the node/test contract for identity removal visibility is slower than currently documented or assumed

## Next Investigation Steps
1. Measure actual disappearance latency with a wider settle window, e.g. 20s, and record time-to-absence.
2. Compare tx acceptance / block inclusion timing to `getAddressInfo` visibility timing.
3. Inspect the PQC remove persistence path for delayed save or eventual-state propagation after accepted tx.
4. Determine whether the correct fix is node-side convergence, or a formally larger settle budget with documented guarantees.

## Likely Fix Areas
- node-side PQC identity removal persistence / convergence after accepted tx
- GCR visibility timing for identity removals
- async post-processing between accepted remove and durable `GCR_Main` update
- any cache / read path that can lag behind accepted committed removal state

## Acceptance Criteria
1. Serialized PQC add/remove cycles converge reliably within the expected settle window on a healthy devnet.
2. Repeated serialized reruns no longer fail with `identity still visible after 8s`, or the expected settle budget is explicitly raised based on measured committed-state behavior.
3. The node/test contract clearly states the expected removal visibility timing so automated tests can enforce the correct threshold.
close_reason: No longer an active testing tasklist item in node repo; superseded by current stabilized coverage and tracker cleanup

comments.author: tcsenpai
comments.text:
Fresh serialized revalidation on the stabilized devnet still reproduces the delayed-removal convergence bug.

Repro:
- `better_testing/scripts/run-scenario.sh gcr_identity_loadgen --run-id validate-gcr-serialized-20260309-01 --env TARGETS=http://node-3:53553 --env CONCURRENCY=1 --env DURATION_SEC=6 --env MIN_LOOP_DELAY_MS=300 --env SETTLE_TIMEOUT_SEC=8 --env SETTLE_POLL_MS=250`

Artifact:
- `better_testing/runs/validate-gcr-serialized-20260309-01/features/gcr/gcr_identity_loadgen.summary.json`

Observed:
- `errorSamples[0].code = CONVERGENCE`
- message: `settle failed: identity still visible after 8s`
- context: `expectPresent=false`, `algorithm=falcon`, single target `http://node-3:53553/`
- `preflight.ok = true`
- `rateLimited = 0`
- `leakedIdentities = []`
- final check `stillPresent = []`

Why this matters:
- this is still reproducing on a clean, cold-boot-fixed cluster without concurrency pressure
- the remove path eventually converges, but not within the current committed-state settle contract

Current best read:
- the node-side visibility lag remains real and distinct from the concurrent rate-limit bug (`node-3qp`) and SDK signer-bleed bug (`node-16j`)

title: Loadgen cannot exercise awardPoints without SUDO sender
description: gcr_points_smoke currently receives RPC 401 Unauthorized sender on local node because awardPoints is protected in server_rpc.ts (PROTECTED_ENDPOINTS). This blocks end-to-end automated points mutation tests for non-sudo wallets.\n\nNeed a testable path: either test-only bypass in non-PROD/devnet, or authenticated test harness that can submit as SUDO, or a dedicated non-protected internal test endpoint.
close_reason: Added authenticated-header support for gcr_points_smoke and optional devnet SUDO_PUBKEY wiring; validated awardPoints end-to-end on 4-node devnet
